title: Measuring response diversity
participants: LiU; CarU; UNamur; SwanU; CSIC; UH; UZH; Monash; ESP; OIST; TN; Zulu; WGov; AdIn; SoilStream; bioAgora; UoL
dcs_involved: DC2; DC4; DC5; DC6; DC8; DC9; DC10; DC11; DC12; DC13
objectives: Develop robust scalable methods for accurately measuring response diversity across ecosystems using non-invasive monitoring experiments theory synthesis and policy analysis.
tasks: Task 1.1 non-invasive spatial and temporal monitoring; Task 1.2 experimental setups and analysis pipelines; Task 1.3 interdisciplinary theoretical and analytical frameworks.

title: The significance of response diversity for stability
participants: LiU; UoL; UNamur; CarU; SwanU; CSIC; UH; UZH; Monash; Lutra; TCD; AdIn; SoilStream; bioAgora
dcs_involved: DC1; DC3; DC6; DC7; DC8; DC9; DC10; DC11; DC12; DC13
objectives: Investigate how response diversity enhances ecological stability across ecosystems and contributes to ecosystem function restoration and sustainability policy.
tasks: Task 2.1 mechanisms linking RD and stability; Task 2.2 relative importance of RD compared with other drivers; Task 2.3 practical applications for restoration and socio-ecological sustainability.

title: The fate of response diversity under environmental change
participants: LiU; UoL; UNamur; CarU; SwanU; UZH; Monash; Lutra; ESP; OIST; TCD; WGov; AdIn; SoilStream
dcs_involved: DC1; DC2; DC3; DC5; DC7; DC10; DC13
objectives: Investigate how intrinsic drivers such as biotic interactions and extrinsic drivers such as environmental and anthropogenic disturbances shape response diversity.
tasks: Task 3.1 biotic interactions and network structure; Task 3.2 chronic press disturbances; Task 3.3 intense pulse disturbances.

title: Training
objectives: Provide interdisciplinary training in research transferable skills and professional development for careers in academia industry and policymaking.
tasks: Recruitment validation; Personal Career Development Plans; training school organisation; monitoring and evaluating the training programme.

title: Communication dissemination and exploitation
objectives: Make researchers' results available to scientific communities industry companies and the public while promoting knowledge on response diversity.
tasks: Project website and communication resources; operational communication and dissemination; events fairs and final showcase.

title: Project management
objectives: Manage recruitment research training finances legal requirements communication reporting and coherent delivery of all project objectives.
tasks: Project coordination; consortium agreement; project office; financial and scientific progress monitoring; External Advisory Board setup.
